name: ROCKWELL MEDICAL, INC.
business: Rockwell Medical develops, manufactures, commercializes, and distributes hemodialysis products for dialysis providers worldwide. The company supplies liquid and dry acid and bicarbonate concentrates to dialysis patients in the United States. Rockwell serves 294 customers including all five leading U.S. dialysis providers.

exposures.sentence: Rockwell Medical is a healthcare company that develops, manufactures, commercializes, and distributes a portfolio of hemodialysis products for dialysis providers worldwide.

catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, we added a single-use bicarbonate cartridge to our hemodialysis concentrates product portfolio that is 510(k) approved by the FDA and comes in two sizes, 720 grams and 900 grams.

catalysts.event: Signed multi-year product purchase agreement with Innovative Renal Care
catalysts.sentence: In July 2025, we signed a multi-year product purchase agreement with Innovative Renal Care, one of the largest dialysis service providers in the United States.

catalysts.event: Added 30 new customers in the western United States
catalysts.sentence: In the fourth quarter of 2025, we added 30 new customers in the western portion of the United States.

catalysts.event: Second Amendment to DaVita agreement extending term to December 31, 2026
catalysts.sentence: On December 31, 2025, the Company and DaVita entered into the Second Amendment, which extended the term of the Amended Agreement by one additional year to December 31, 2026.

catalysts.event: Ceased manufacturing at South Carolina facility
catalysts.sentence: The Company concluded manufacturing at that facility in the third quarter of 2025 as part of its ongoing efforts to streamline operations and improve efficiency.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. hemodialysis concentrates market projected to grow to $560 million by 2028 from $450 million in 2024
tamClaims.sentence: Based on an independent research report that the Company commissioned from L.E.K. Consulting LLC in 2022, the hemodialysis concentrates market in the U.S. is projected to grow to approximately $560 million by 2028, up from $450 million in 2024.
